title: austrocylindropuntia floccosa subsp. crispicrinitus
intro: Austrocylindropuntia floccosa subsp. crispicrinitus, also known as the 'Hairy Cholla', is a species of cactus that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. It is a type of cactus that has long, thin stems covered in fine, soft hairs and produces yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and reducing pain. The Hairy Cholla can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and salves to harness its health benefits, including promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation.
